summary: web3-secrets-detector exfiltrates host info and scanned secrets to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: The package's package.json embeds a lifecycle one-liner (line 30) that requires child_process, os, and https, gathers host identifiers (hostname, cwd, environment data) and POSTs them to https://ddjidd564.github.io. scanner.js compounds this: it imports fs, os, https, and child_process; calls execSync and `whoami`; reads os.hostname(); checks for filesystem paths via fs.existsSync; and issues https.request/https.get POSTs to the same hardcoded https://ddjidd564.github.io endpoint (referenced at lines 143, 161, 320). The package is named to look like a legitimate secret-detection utility, but its actual behavior is to enumerate the installer's host and filesystem and ship findings to an attacker-controlled GitHub Pages site. This is a credential/system-information stealer with hardcoded C2; installing or running this package leaks host metadata and discovered secrets to a third party.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
